# Questions in Livestream 161

Live Stream #161: All The Second-Born Questions

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=961" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:01</a> | When was Amenhotep II born? | He says there is no direct birth notice and reasons instead from the mummy's estimated age and the span of his reign. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=1219" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:19</a> | Why is the city of palms listed between the Negev and Zoar? | He says Deuteronomy 34 is not a straight-line itinerary but a sweeping visual arc from Moses' viewpoint. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=1572" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:12</a> | Could Dr. Falk see an aurora, and how does that relate to Joshua's long day? | He says he saw only a gray smudge through city light pollution, then ties the Joshua reference to an astronomical/calendar discussion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=1744" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:04</a> | Would there be an aurora in Egypt? | He says that is unlikely because Egypt is too close to the equator for ordinary auroral visibility. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=1960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:40</a> | If a firstborn heir dies before his father but leaves a son, does the son inherit or does a brother inherit? | He says the brother normally becomes the next heir as the nearest inheriting male. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=2095" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:55</a> | Should Judges 4:4 be translated as Deborah leading Israel or judging Israel? | He says the key Hebrew verb is "judging," while also describing Deborah as a prophetess. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=2409" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:09</a> | Is Deborah the one judge who is never rebuked and only praised in Scripture? | He says she is a good judge and is not rebuked, but she is not unique in that respect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=2470" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:10</a> | Do bad judges such as Jephthah and Samson contradict Hebrews 11:33-34 and 11:38? | He says Hebrews 11 should not be treated as simple praise of every listed person's whole life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=2892" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:12</a> | Is Korah's reasoning in Numbers 16:3 analogous to egalitarian use of Galatians 3:28? | He says the two arguments are not directly analogous and need to be kept in their own contexts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=3401" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:41</a> | Which ancient peoples used a seven-day week? | He says the seven-day cycle comes out of Semitic culture, unlike Egyptian ten-day and Roman eight-day systems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=3645" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:45</a> | What are Dr. Falk's thoughts on Niebuhr? | He frames Niebuhr through German dialectic and says that method can be hard for North American readers to follow. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=3931" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:31</a> | How should readers understand the numbers in Numbers 1? | He says scholars agree the census numbers are not being used in the modern straightforward way, but the precise interpretation remains disputed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=4336" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:16</a> | Did the Assyrians deport all of northern Israel or mainly the upper class? | He says Assyrian deportation aimed at breaking up clans rather than following the later Babylonian elite-focused pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=4770" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:30</a> | Why do so many Europeans claim that they are descended from the Israelites? | He connects the claim to later Jewish dispersal after the Roman suppression of revolts, not to the Assyrian deportation itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=4935" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:15</a> | Is there reason to think some members of the northern tribes fled to Judah during the Assyrian conquest? | He says yes, at least some probably did even though the Assyrian sweep was still severe. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=4986" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:06</a> | Which ancient Near Eastern nations were made up of city-states? | He says essentially all of them were at some point, depending on the stage of nation formation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=5354" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:14</a> | Can Dr. Falk elaborate on baptism and communion not being solely symbolic acts? | He explains communion through gathered presence and covenantal participation rather than mere symbolism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=5784" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:24</a> | How should Christians react to false prophets or teachers who attack the faith? | He says Christians should take the danger seriously and respond by Christ's own principles rather than mere tolerance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=6383" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:23</a> | Did Joseph replace the current vizier? | He says probably; viziers were not lifetime appointments and could be replaced. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=6453" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:33</a> | How should we understand human sacrifice in Egypt? | He says the subject is controversial and many once-proposed examples have been discounted as scholarship has developed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=6624" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:24</a> | What does Dr. Falk think of Jordan Peterson's view of Christianity and the Bible? | He says he is not impressed and describes Peterson's approach as Jungian rather than a strong account of Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=8301" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:21</a> | Are the sons of Korah mentioned in Psalm 42 likely descendants of the Korah from Numbers 16? | He says the title likely reflects a Korahite group, but he distinguishes it from assuming a simple direct link to the rebellion figure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=8914" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:34</a> | What are Dr. Falk's thoughts on the Gavin and Trent slavery debate? | He says the debate fallout developed as he expected and uses it to criticize how some critics frame objections. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=9206" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:26</a> | How should the appearance of Asher or Isher in Egyptian topographical lists be explained? | He says topographical lists normally name places rather than tribes, so the identification needs caution. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=10755" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:15</a> | What symbolism did the plagues represent? | He begins answering through the plague narratives and their symbolic force, with cross-discussion from Hebrews. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=10977" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:57</a> | Did the Nile literally become blood in the first plague? | He leans against literal blood and prefers blood-like appearance or properties within ancient idiom. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=11196" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:36</a> | What are the meanings of other names listed in Numbers 16? | He says many of the names are uncertain, so only a few can be discussed with confidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=11320" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:40</a> | Why was Hatshepsut not mentioned in the early-date Exodus playlist, and how should one answer the claim that Senenmut was Moses? | He says Senenmut's Egyptian parents and other evidence make the identification with Moses untenable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=12094" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:34</a> | What references should someone use for slavery by biblical scholars? | He says Richard Averbeck has written on the subject and recommends starting with good commentaries and bibliographies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=12192" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:12</a> | What kinds of favors could someone ask from a pharaoh? | He says people might ask for a job or honorific status, but not vulgar material favors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=12378" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:18</a> | Why not ask the king for stuff? | He says asking for material goods would be viewed as vulgar and could offend the king. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=12544" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:04</a> | Could the king arbitrarily give someone the death penalty? | He says no; even without written law, Egyptian society had legal traditions that constrained the king. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=12706" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:46</a> | Are there comprehensive commentaries on the historicity of Daniel in its cultural context? | He recommends the Tyndale House essay volume on problems in Daniel and names Wiseman, Fairman, and Kitchen as contributors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=12876" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:36</a> | Could Dr. Falk explain the synchronism of Neferhotep I and Hammurabi as "Kish"? | He says there is not much for a full video, but points to the relevant stela and citation trail. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=13758" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:18</a> | Is Richard Dawkins essentially a Christian now? | He says no; liking some trappings of Christian culture does not make Dawkins a Christian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=13806" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:06</a> | What was the economy like around the time of Moses? | He describes it as a barter economy where metals were weighed and many people were paid in food or tangible goods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=13943" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:23</a> | What are Dr. Falk's thoughts on the Oxford Annotated Study Bible? | He says he has not used it and does not comment seriously on a Bible without living with a physical copy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=14901" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:08:21</a> | What are Dr. Falk's thoughts on the claim that Christianity leads to nihilism? | He says that is a distortion of Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=15654" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:20:54</a> | What does Dr. Falk think of Esoterica's claims about Israel being polytheistic and Yahweh being the son of El? | He says he has not watched the channel, affirms Israel often practiced polytheism, and distinguishes that from the underlying theology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2f1Teze-G7Y?t=16413" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:33:33</a> | How should artists accurately describe Satan? | He says Satan should be portrayed as destructive, jealous, and already defeated rather than as a misunderstood ruler. |
